previous_statement: P(I)=I. By GT-positive-unital Phi(I)=I, so S(I)=(2Phi-I)(I)=I, hence S^2(I)=I and U(I)=(S^2-I)(I)=0. As R=sum_k C(-1/2,k)U^k with C(-1/2,0)=1, R(I)=I; thus sgn(S)(I)=S R(I)=S(I)=I and P(I)=(I+sgn(S))(I)/2=I. Uses node 1.2.
new_statement: P(I)=I. By GT-positive-unital Phi(I)=I, so S(I)=(2Phi-I)(I)=I, hence S^2(I)=I and U(I)=(S^2-I)(I)=0. By GT-funcalc, R=f(S^2)=sum_{n>=0} a_n U^n with constant term a_0=f(x0)=f(1)=1^(-1/2)=1 (value of f at the Taylor center). Since U(I)=0, U^n(I)=0 for all n>=1, so R(I)=a_0 I=I; thus sgn(S)(I)=S R(I)=S(I)=I and P(I)=(I+sgn(S))(I)/2=I. Uses node 1.2.
